objective: Improve the agentic-dev plans-to-contracts workflow by making plan/contract state inventory explicit before execution without weakening approval, scope, or verification gates.

experiments.change_summary: No mutation. Baseline measurement of existing plan, contract, capture, projection, and hook surfaces.
experiments.reasoning: The current workflow preserves approval and isolation, but generated artifacts do not force a concrete state inventory before execution and do not consolidate workflow verification evidence.
experiments.result_notes: Useful baseline: 14/20. Strong gates already exist; the pressure point is missing explicit inventory and representative verification on the files agents actually read before implementation.
experiments.failing_patterns: Agents can rely on generic plan prose and contract placeholders instead of naming active plan, contract, review, notes, todo, checks, allowed paths, and workflow checks before editing.

experiments.description: add workflow inventory to plan and contract surfaces
experiments.change_summary: Added explicit workflow inventory to generated plans, captured plans, contracts, fallback templates, planning facade, docs, and assembly guidance; retained approval and contract gates.
experiments.reasoning: The article/autoresearch lesson is to make the target function representative and keep useful instructions near the action point. The smallest coherent change is to place inventory and verification ownership in plans/contracts, not to remove gates or add a new abstraction.
experiments.result_notes: Targeted regression passed: bun test tests/helper-scripts.test.ts tests/scaffold-parity.test.ts tests/output-parity.test.ts tests/agents-assembly.test.ts. One line-budget failure appeared on the first run and was fixed by compressing AGENTS wording.
experiments.failing_patterns: No remaining scored failures for this mutation.

eval_breakdown.name: inventory_first
eval_breakdown.question: Does the workflow require a concrete plan/contract inventory before implementation?

eval_breakdown.name: approval_boundary
eval_breakdown.question: Does the workflow preserve Draft to Approved to Executing gates?

eval_breakdown.name: artifact_linkage
eval_breakdown.question: Are plans, contracts, reviews, notes, todos, and checks linked by stable paths?

eval_breakdown.name: representative_verification
eval_breakdown.question: Does verification measure the real workflow rather than only a generic code check?

eval_breakdown.name: concurrency_isolation
eval_breakdown.question: Does the workflow avoid one stale Draft plan globally blocking unrelated planning or execution?

test_prompts: A user asks to plan a workflow change while an older Draft plan already exists.
A user pastes an approved plan and asks to implement it immediately.
An agent is about to edit files under an active tasks/contracts/*.contract.md scope.
A session resumes with plans/, tasks/todo.md, tasks/contracts/, review, notes, and checks files present.
